methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $575M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
VulcanForms is a late-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by John Hart that has raised $575M+ to date. The broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector includes 9 peer companies (1 public, 8 private), with notable private comparables including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting narrower screening criteria, limiting the applicability of multiples-based approaches.

A stage-progression methodology proves the only reliable triangulation path given data constraints. Using total capital raised as a valuation proxy of $575M and applying a typical late-stage progression multiplier of 1.2x yields an implied valuation of $690M. This reflects the capital already deployed and expected step-up upon advancement to the next financing stage. The methodology is grounded in observable late-stage fund-to-valuation patterns, though it relies on the assumption that $575M raised approximates fair entry value.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $586M (low) to $793M (high), with a median of $690M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty: the absence of tightly comparable late-stage robotics deals limits cross-validation, and stage-progression mechanics do not account for company-specific unit economics, growth trajectory, or sector-specific risk factors. Acquisition of additional comparable transaction data or deployment of discounted cash flow analysis would materially improve conviction in any final valuation conclusion.
